name: the-inner-game-of-tennis
description: W. Timothy Gallwey's The Inner Game of Tennis — a performance psychology toolkit revealing that the greatest opponent is the mind itself (Self 1 vs Self 2), and that excellence comes not from trying harder but from quieting judgment, trusting the body, and letting natural capability emerge.
useCase: Master the mental side of peak performance — quiet the inner critic, trust your body's natural capability, and learn through non-judgmental awareness. Applicable to sports, business, creativity, and any performance domain.
knownRisks: The book uses tennis as a metaphor — non-tennis readers may need patience with the sports context
The Self 1/Self 2 framework is a simplification and may not capture all aspects of performance psychology
Trusting the body is not appropriate for all situations (e.g., learning dangerous physical skills)
